description: A Haas effect (simulate stereo position using delay)

parameterGroups.label: Stereo position
parameterGroups.rationale: Single parameter controlling the perceived stereo position via inter-channel delay.

gaps.question: How is the delay implemented? Does it use a delay buffer? Is interpolation used for sub-sample delay? What is the maximum delay time and how does Position map to delay time?
gaps.context: The existing doc mentions a hardcoded 20ms maximum. Need to confirm and understand the Position-to-delay mapping.

gaps.question: How does the node handle stereo? Does it delay one channel and leave the other dry? What happens with mono input or >2 channels?
gaps.context: Haas effect requires at least stereo. Need to document channel requirements and behaviour.

gaps.question: Position ranges -1 to 1. At Position=0, is delay zero on both channels (bypass)? At Position=-1, which channel is delayed? Is the mapping linear?
gaps.context: Users need to understand the left/right semantics of the Position parameter.

gaps.question: IsPolyphonic is true. Does the node maintain per-voice delay buffers?
gaps.context: Per-voice delay buffers have significant memory implications.

notes: Existing doc mentions hardcoded 20ms maximum delay. The Haas effect works by applying a short delay (under ~35ms) to one channel, creating the perception of stereo directionality. Step size of 0.1 suggests coarse control. Only 1 parameter makes this a simple node.
